summary: Fuzz plan covers TC-ROLE-200 against all 7 role endpoints via schemathesis schema-based property testing. Schema source (http://localhost:9999/openapi.json) and auth strategy (token header injection via before_call hook reusing get_admin_token) are both concrete and resolved. Cleanup via session-scoped role-id-set diff. Related functional cases are mapped (TC-ROLE-005/006/010/011). schemathesis 3.39.16 was installed in-session per user instruction (resolves prior B-FUZZ-01). Pytest auto-downgraded 9.0.3 to 8.4.2; existing tests still collect cleanly. Risk medium because NR-FUZZ-01 / NR-FUZZ-02 represent unresolved product behavior questions about how the controller should respond to weakly-typed inputs (api_infos, long names) — these are inspect-time classification questions, not plan-time blockers. No blockers.

needs_review.question: RoleUpdateMenusApis.api_infos is list[dict] weakly-typed in OpenAPI schema. If fuzz produces api_infos with non-existent (path,method) tuple, controller calls Api.filter(...).first() → may bind None and raise. Should this be classified as product_issue (defensive None-check expected) or expected_baseline (caller's fault)?
needs_review.reason: NR-FUZZ-01; cannot decide without product team decision. Inspect classifier needs this to label fuzz failures correctly. Non-blocking at plan stage; impacts only failure classification, not test execution.

needs_review.question: RoleCreate.name has no max-length constraint in schema. Schemathesis will generate very long strings; SQLite/MySQL may raise integrity errors → 5xx. Should this be classified as product_issue or expected_baseline?
needs_review.reason: NR-FUZZ-02; same classification question as FUZZ-PLAN-REVIEW-001. Non-blocking at plan stage.

needs_review.question: uv add 'schemathesis>=3.30,<4' downgraded pytest from 9.0.3 to 8.4.2. Acceptable side-effect?
needs_review.reason: NR-FUZZ-03; verified existing test suites collect cleanly under 8.4.2. pytest 8 is the current stable. Non-blocking.

needs_review.question: Cleanup uses role-id-set diff (snapshot pre/post). Captures any leaked role regardless of name. Acceptable robustness?
needs_review.reason: NR-FUZZ-04; chosen over name-prefix sweep because schemathesis-generated names are too random to filter. Diff is simpler and more correct. Non-blocking.
